q.en: About how much of our lives do people spend asleep?
q.zh: 人们一生中大约有多少时间是在睡眠中度过的？

explain.en: The 'Wowser!' box says: 'people spend about a third of their lives asleep.'
explain.zh: “Wowser!” 方框写道：“人们一生中约有三分之一的时间在睡觉。”

q.en: What substance builds up in the body during the day and triggers sleep?
q.zh: 白天体内会累积并触发睡意的物质是什么？

explain.en: The book states: 'a substance called adenosine... triggers sleep' as it builds up while the body uses energy.
explain.zh: 书中说：“一种叫腺苷的物质……会触发睡眠”，它在身体消耗能量时不断累积。

q.en: What does the brain do with memories while we sleep?
q.zh: 我们睡觉时，大脑如何处理记忆？

options.en: It shuffles memories from short-term to long-term memory
options.zh: 它把记忆从短期记忆“搬运”到长期记忆

explain.en: The book says: 'The brain must move information from short-term memory to long-term memory. This shuffle happens while we sleep.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“大脑必须把信息从短期记忆移到长期记忆。这种搬运发生在我们睡眠时。”

q.en: Why is sleep especially important for athletes?
q.zh: 为什么睡眠对运动员尤其重要？

options.en: It restores body tissues and builds new cells broken down in training
options.zh: 它能修复身体组织，重建训练中损耗的细胞

options.en: It makes them run faster instantly
options.zh: 它让他们立刻跑得更快

options.en: It replaces the need to eat

explain.en: The book explains sleep 'restores body tissues' because during training 'they constantly break down cells and tissues.'
explain.zh: 书中解释睡眠“修复身体组织”，因为训练中“他们不断损耗细胞和组织”。

q.en: According to the glossary, what is the 'immune system'?
q.zh: 根据词汇表，“immune system（免疫系统）”是什么？

options.en: The cells and tissues that work to fight infection
options.zh: 致力于对抗感染的细胞和组织

options.en: The part of the brain that controls dreams

explain.en: The glossary defines immune system as 'a system that moves antibodies through the body to fight infection.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将免疫系统定义为“在身体中运送抗体以对抗感染的系统”。

q.zh: REM（快速眼动）睡眠期间会发生什么？

options.en: Closed eyes move rapidly and the brain is almost as active as when awake
options.zh: 闭着的眼睛快速转动，大脑活跃程度几乎和清醒时一样

explain.en: The book says during REM 'closed eyes move rapidly from side to side' and 'Brain activity... are almost at waking levels.'
explain.zh: 书中说 REM 期间“闭着的眼睛快速左右移动”，且“大脑活动……几乎达到清醒时的水平”。

q.en: What are the two main types of sleep described in the book?
q.zh: 书中描述的两种主要睡眠类型是什么？

options.zh: REM 睡眠和 NREM 睡眠

explain.en: The book states: 'There are two types of sleep—rapid eye movement (REM) sleep and non-rapid eye movement (NREM) sleep.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“睡眠有两种——快速眼动（REM）睡眠和非快速眼动（NREM）睡眠。”

q.en: Which sleep problem means suddenly falling asleep at any time?
q.zh: 哪种睡眠问题指的是随时突然入睡？

explain.en: The book says: 'Narcolepsy is almost the opposite of insomnia. People with narcolepsy may suddenly fall asleep at any time.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“发作性嗜睡症几乎与失眠相反。患者可能随时突然入睡。”

q.en: Which habit does the book recommend for good sleep?
q.zh: 书中推荐哪种好习惯来保证好睡眠？

options.en: Go to bed and wake up at the same time, even on weekends
options.zh: 每天同一时间睡觉和起床，即便在周末

options.en: Use a smartphone right before bed
Eat a big meal at bedtime

explain.en: The book advises: 'Try to go to bed and wake up at the same time each day. This helps keep your body on schedule.'
explain.zh: 书中建议：“尽量每天同一时间睡觉和起床。这有助于让身体保持规律。”

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To explain why sleep is important to good health
options.zh: 解释为什么睡眠对身体健康很重要

explain.en: The Focus Question is 'How is sleep important to good health?' and the text informs readers about sleep's role.
explain.zh: 书中的“焦点问题”是“睡眠对健康为何重要？”，全文都在向读者说明睡眠的作用。

prompt.en: Keep a one-night sleep diary: write down when you went to bed, when you woke, and how you felt. Then suggest one habit from the book you could improve.
prompt.zh: 写一晚的睡眠日记：记下你几点睡、几点醒、感觉如何。然后提出书中你可以改进的一个习惯。

en: Use the book's tips, like a steady schedule and less screen time before bed.
Notice whether you felt rested and link it to your habits.
zh: 运用书中的建议，比如固定作息、睡前少看屏幕。
留意自己是否休息充分，并把感受和习惯联系起来。
